title: Clerk API keys should not be disclosed
description: Clerk is a modern authentication and user management platform. Clerk credentials include publishable keys (frontend), secret keys (backend), and webhook signing secrets. Secret keys provide full access to user management including creating/deleting users and managing sessions.
recommendation: Remove Clerk secret keys from source code. Store in environment variables. Rotate immediately at dashboard.clerk.com.
impact: Full user management access including reading user data, creating/deleting accounts, managing sessions, and accessing authentication logs.

checks.message: Clerk secret key detected (sk_live_ or sk_test_ prefix). Clerk backend secret keys provide full administrative access to your authentication system including all user records and profile data, session management and revocation, user creation and deletion, organization management, and webhook configuration. Exposing this key allows complete account takeover of your authentication infrastructure. Rotate immediately at dashboard.clerk.com.
Clerk secret key detected in variable assignment. Clerk manages authentication and user data. Rotate immediately at dashboard.clerk.com.
